1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 076 — **repair round**: two parallel lanes restoring compilation in `Differentials.lean` and `Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean`).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the five-phase build-out plan. **Phase C is re-opened iter-076** per user directive 2026-05-12 — replaces the prior "deferred indefinitely" framing with a four-step (C0→C3) Mathlib gap-fill plan. The user's directive: "This project aims at filling mathlib gaps, therefore you should not defer it, instead you should trace a route to fill the gap and close the sorries of phase C, as you already did for the other phases by introducing all the necessary lemmas and definitions, subagents might be useful for this."
18	
19	The blueprint chapter `Modules_Monoidal.tex` (Phase C step C0 informal content) was written this iter; `Picard_Functor.tex` was softened with the C0–C3 roadmap; `content.tex` updated. The Lean-side scaffolding for the new file `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` is deferred to iter-077 (refactor subagent), because iter-076's first priority is repair.
20	
21	After verifying with `lean_diagnostic_messages` this plan pass, the actual project state is:
22	
23	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-076 plan |
24	|---|---|---|---|---|
25	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | **6** | **NO** (5 errors) | A | **Repair Lane 2** |
26	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | **6** | **NO** (12 errors) | B | **Repair Lane 1** |
27	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | C (re-opened) | off-limits this iter |
28	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep (re-opened) | off-limits this iter |
29	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E **DONE** | off-limits |
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
31	| **Total syntactic** | **14** | — | | |
32	
33	**Critical correction iter-076.** The persistent PROGRESS / STRATEGY note "build environment broken since iter-069" is **stale**. `lean_diagnostic_messages` works correctly and returns useful errors; `lean_leansearch` works; the LSP is responsive. What is actually broken is the *project source*: in-flight prover bodies in `Differentials.lean` (Steps 7+ of `_structure.h_zero` and the `d_target` construction inside `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`) and in `BasicOpenCech.lean` (the smul transport for `f_R`/`g_R` and the `h_diff_pi_smul_g` analogue) have accumulated multiple type errors and heartbeat timeouts. The iter-074/075 dispatches' "commit credible tactics without LSP verification" policy was rooted in this stale assumption and is what produced the current broken state.
34	
35	This iteration corrects the framing: the lanes use `lean_diagnostic_messages` to verify each tactic-edit before committing. The user policy "no `lean_run_code` pre-validation of candidate bodies" stays — but `lean_diagnostic_messages` on edits-already-on-disk is NOT pre-validation; it's the standard development feedback loop.
36	
37	## Current Objectives
38	
39	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file. **Both lanes are repair-only**; no new feature work; new sorries are acceptable wherever an in-flight tactic chain cannot be repaired in-iteration.
40	
41	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — REPAIR (restore compilation, preserve 6 sorries)
42	
43	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries (L122, L604, L617, L662, L679, L821), 823 LOC. **File does not compile.** 12 errors reported by `lean_diagnostic_messages` this plan pass.
44	
45	**Goal.** Make the file compile cleanly (no errors). Preserve the 6 residual sorries at the listed lines. Where an in-flight tactic chain cannot be repaired in-iteration, **roll the surrounding `by`-block back to `by sorry`** with a one-line `-- ITER-076: <issue>` comment.
46	
47	**Specific errors to repair** (from `lean_diagnostic_messages` this plan pass):
48	
49	1. **L244** (`cotangentExactSeqAlpha`'s `d_target.d` constructor) — unsolved additivity goal. The `(by intro a b; simp)` argument leaves `D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (a + b)) = D_X.d (… a) + D_X.d (… b)`. **Repair**: replace with `(by intro a b; rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_add, D_X.d_add])`.
50	
51	2. **L248** (`d_target.d_mul`) — rewrite pattern not present after `dsimp`. **Repair**: insert `show D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (a * b)) = a • D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom b) + b • D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom a)` before the `rw`, then `rw [(f.c.app U).hom.map_mul, D_X.d_mul]; rfl`.
52	
53	3. **L256** (`d_target.d_map`) — `Function expected at ConcreteCategory.hom h`. **Repair**: tag the `h` type, `have := congr_arg (fun h : _ ⟶ _ => (ConcreteCategory.hom h) x) (f.c.naturality f')` (with an explicit hom-type or via a `let h_eq := f.c.naturality f'` pre-binding).
54	
55	4. **L258** — same shape as L248. **Repair**: insert an explicit `show` to commit the `(f.c.app V).hom (Y.presheaf.map f' x)` form before the `rw`.
56	
57	5. **L266** (`d_target.d_app`'s `hcomm` step) — `rfl` claim fails for `φ_g' ≫ f.c = adj_f.unit ≫ (pushforward).map φ_fg'`. This identity is not definitionally true; it requires the adjunction-coherence at the composition `(f ≫ g).base = g.base ≫ f.base`. **Repair**: roll back the `d_target.d_app` proof block to `sorry` with a comment, OR derive `hcomm` via `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality` + `Equiv.symm_apply_eq` instead of `rfl`. The simpler in-iteration fix is the rollback.
58	
59	6. **L297, L342, L343** (`cotangentExactSeqBeta`) — `whnf` / tactic-execution heartbeat timeouts at 200000 heartbeats. The `set adj_fg; set η` chain at L304–L306 is heavy. **Repair**: prepend `set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in` to `cotangentExactSeqBeta`'s definition, OR roll back `cotangentExactSeqBeta`'s body to `sorry` (it was originally `sorry` and the closure attempted in iter-069 produced this chain). The simpler in-iteration fix is the rollback — `cotangentExactSeqBeta` was closed iter-069 by an earlier attempt; the current body is a re-attempt that doesn't fit in 200k heartbeats.
60	
61	7. **L332** (`cotangentExactSeqBeta`'s `hη.h5`) — `No goals to be solved` on `exact h4`. **Repair**: remove the redundant `exact h4` line (the prior `simp only / erw [h6]` chain has already closed the goal).
62	
63	8. **L381–L384** — `Unknown identifier cotangentExactSeqBeta` cascade. **Resolves automatically** once L297/L342 are repaired.
64	
65	9. **L452** (`cotangentExactSeq_structure`'s `h_zero` setup) — `simp made no progress`. **Repair**: replace `simp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]` with `dsimp only [SheafOfModules.comp_val, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]`, or drop the line.
66	
67	10. **L645** (`cotangent_exact_sequence`) — `Unknown identifier cotangentExactSeq_structure`. **Resolves automatically** once the upstream is repaired.
68	
69	**Fallback.** Any block that cannot be repaired in-iteration: replace with `by sorry` + `-- ITER-076: <issue>` comment. **Goal is compilation cleanness, not closing more sorries.**
70	
71	**Sorry-count budget.** Starts at 6; the repaired file should have **at most 8** sorries (allow +2 for in-flight rollback).
72	
73	**Hard constraints**:
74	- **No new project-local helper lemmas.** Per user feedback memory ("no chains of thin helpers across iterations").
75	- **No new axioms.**
76	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** (user policy). `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each save IS allowed (it's not pre-validation; it's the standard dev feedback loop).
77	
78	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex` (unchanged this iter).
79	
80	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — REPAIR (restore compilation, preserve 6 sorries)
81	
82	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries (L495, L819, L847, L1166, L1181, L1260), 1270 LOC. **File does not compile.** 5 errors reported by `lean_diagnostic_messages` this plan pass.
83	
84	**Goal.** Make the file compile cleanly. Preserve the 6 residual sorries at the listed lines. Same fallback policy as Lane 1.
85	
86	**Specific errors to repair**:
87	
88	1. **L952** — unsolved goal: `Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ fun i ↦ ModuleCat.of k ↑(C.left.presheaf.obj (…))) = Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₃)`. The `h_mod_pi₃` setup derives the instance via `Pi.module` but the underlying type defeq doesn't close. **Repair**: insert `convert h_mod_pi₃` or `change Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₃) from h_mod_pi₃`; or wrap the line in `letI : Module ↑R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₃) := h_mod_pi₃`.
89	
90	2. **L1088** — `simp made no progress`. In `h_diff_pi_smul_f`, the `simp only [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply, Finset.smul_sum]` after the bulk simp on L1080–L1087 fires on a target where the lemmas don't apply (or have already fired). **Repair**: replace `simp only` with `try simp only`, or drop the line entirely.
91	
92	3. **L1170, L1171** — type mismatches in `h_diff_pi_smul_g`. `(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (e₂.symm (r • y))` has type `↑scK₀.X₃`, but `e₃` expects `↑(∏ᶜ Z₃)`. The statement at L1167–L1171 was patterned on `h_diff_pi_smul_f` but the wrong type-bridge was used. **Repair**: roll back the body of `h_diff_pi_smul_g` to a bare `sorry` (preserving the `letI`s and the statement shape via `change`/explicit annotations), OR fix the statement so the `e₃`-images type-check.
93	
94	4. **L1182** — `isDefEq` heartbeat timeout (≥ 200000 heartbeats) in `h_diff_pi_smul_g`'s `try` chain. **Repair**: remove the `try funext j; try simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` chain at L1179–L1180; replace the body with `intro r y; sorry`.
95	
96	5. **L412** — `whnf` heartbeat timeout. **Investigate**: read the context at L412; if it's inside the dead-end substep (L495 territory), the body may have been recently bloated. Wrap with `set_option maxHeartbeats 400000 in` locally OR roll back the offending block.
97	
98	6. **L1205, L1213** (cascade from above) — unsolved goals + `whnf` timeout in `f_R.map_smul'`/`g_R.map_smul'`'s `calc` chain. **Repair**: if necessary, roll back the `map_smul'` bodies of `f_R` and `g_R` to `by intro r x; sorry`.
99	
100	**Fallback.** Same as Lane 1: replace problematic `by`-blocks with `by sorry` + `-- ITER-076` comment.
101	
102	**Sorry-count budget.** Starts at 6; the repaired file should have **at most 9** sorries (allow +3 for the potential `f_R`/`g_R`/`h_diff_pi_smul_g` rollbacks).
103	
104	**Hard constraints**: same as Lane 1.
105	
106	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § *Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on affine basic-open covers*; also `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`.
107	
108	## Off-limits this iteration

111	- `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` (0 sorries) — DONE iter-073.

116	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` — does not exist; refactor subagent creates it in iter-077.

119	## Verification (pre-dispatch, iter-076 plan pass)
120	
121	| Check | Status |
122	|---|---|

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
83	
84	**Format:**
85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
88	
89	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
90	### Attempt 1
91	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
92	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
93	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
94	
95	### Attempt 2
96	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
97	- **Result:** RESOLVED
98	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
99	
100	## helper_bijective (line 78)
101	### Attempt 1
102	- **Approach:** Function.Bijective.comp
103	- **Result:** IN PROGRESS — stuck on surjectivity
104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
130	
131	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
132	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
133	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
134	   - Concrete next step for the next session
135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
136	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)

1	/-

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCover
7	
8	/-!
9	# Basic-open cover wrappers and the substantive Čech acyclicity theorem
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 3) contains the `basicOpenCover` family
12	of definitions and identification lemmas (iter-054), the producer
13	`hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` (iter-055), the
14	`cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt` lemma, and the
15	substantive theorem `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
16	(iter-056 → iter-062). The latter currently carries two labelled substep
17	sorries plus the iter-062 `h_a_fun` scaffolding.
18	
19	This is cohort 3 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	* `BasicOpenCech.lean` (this file): basic-open cover wrappers + the
24	  substantive Čech acyclicity theorem.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.

418	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
419	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
420	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the

425	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on

427	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
428	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.

580	    -- `R`-linear via the `Module R ((K.sc n).X i)`-structure obtained from
581	    -- the cochain factor's natural `Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`-module structure
582	    -- precomposed with the algebra map `R → Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`).
583	    -- The required input is, for each `f ∈ s`, a `Function.Exact`
584	    -- statement on the localised cochain factors at `f`. By the
585	    -- cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
586	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
587	    -- commutation), the localised `(K.sc n).X i [1/f]` identifies with the
588	    -- slice-cover Čech `.X i` at the same degree, and consequently the
589	    -- localised differential identifies with the slice-cover differential.
590	    -- This identification + `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` transports
591	    -- `h_a_fun f` (which is the slice-cover Čech `Function.Exact` at
592	    -- degree `n`) to `Function.Exact` of the localised differential.
593	    -- The infinite-set subtlety (`s` is `Set`, not `Finset`) is sidestepped
594	    -- because `Ideal.span s = ⊤` already implies a *finite* subset spans
595	    -- the unit ideal — `exact_of_isLocalized_span` only consumes the per-`r`
596	    -- localised exactness data and the spanning hypothesis, not finiteness
597	    -- of `s` directly.
598	    -- ===========================================================================
599	    -- Substep (b1) — install the `Module Γ(C.left, U)` instances on each
600	    -- cochain factor `(K.sc n).X i`.
601	    -- ===========================================================================
602	    -- The cochain factor `K.X i = ∏_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))`
603	    -- carries a `Γ(C.left, U)`-module structure pointwise, since each
604	    -- `Γ(C.left, V)` for `V ≤ U` is a `Γ(C.left, U)`-algebra via the
605	    -- restriction map (iter-058's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le` supplies
606	    -- the inclusion `V ≤ U`, iter-006's `algebraSection` supplies the
607	    -- algebra structure on each factor).
608	    --
609	    -- ===========================================================================
610	    -- Substep (b2) — exhibit `slice_K.X i` as `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`
611	    -- of `(K.sc n).X i` for each `f ∈ s`.
612	    -- ===========================================================================
613	    -- The cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
614	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
615	    -- commutation) gives
616	    --   `slice_K.X i = ∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰_slice(x k))`
617	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k) ⊓ D(f))`
618	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))[1/f]`        (iter-058 + iter-059)
619	    --                = `(∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k)))[1/f]`     (product-loc commutation)
620	    --                = `(K.X i)[1/f]`.
621	    -- The pointwise localisation map (component-wise restriction map
622	    -- `Γ(C.left, V) → Γ(C.left, V')` for `V' = V ⊓ D(f) ≤ V`) is the canonical
623	    -- localisation map and an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instance.
624	    --
625	    -- ===========================================================================
626	    -- Substep (c) — apply `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ... h_a_fun`.
627	    -- ===========================================================================
628	    -- With the data from (b1) and (b2), `exact_of_isLocalized_span` packages
629	    -- the per-`f` exactness `h_a_fun f` into the global `Function.Exact`
630	    -- conclusion. The remaining work is purely the unification of the per-`f`
631	    -- input shape with the conclusion of `IsLocalizedModule.map ... F`
632	    -- via the iso from (b2) and `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`.
633	    -- ===========================================================================
634	    -- Iter-063 progress: explicit finite-subspanning extraction + obstruction
635	    -- diagnosis.
636	    -- ===========================================================================
637	    -- The localisation-commutes-with-products subtlety identified by the
638	    -- iter-061 prover (Attempt 3) is genuine and severe: for the cochain
639	    -- factor `K.X i = ∏ᶜ_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} P.obj (op (∏ᶜ_k 𝒰(x k)))` in
640	    -- `ModuleCat k` (where `P` is the underlying presheaf of `toModuleKSheaf C`),
641	    -- the index set `Fin (i+1) → s` is *infinite* when `s` is infinite, and
642	    -- `LocalizedModule (powers f) (∏ᶜ_x M_x) ≄ ∏ᶜ_x LocalizedModule (powers f) M_x`
643	    -- in general for infinite products. So `slice_K.X i = ∏_x Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f))`
644	    -- is NOT literally `(K.X i)[1/f]` — the natural map between them is
645	    -- *not* an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`. Iter-062's substep (b2) plan
646	    -- ("install `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instances on the product")
647	    -- requires reducing to a finite index-set first.
648	    --
649	    -- Iter-063 lands the finite-subspanning extraction as a structural fact
650	    -- inside the proof: `Ideal.span s = ⊤` implies the existence of a
651	    -- *finite* subset `s₀ ⊆ s` with `Ideal.span ↑s₀ = ⊤` via Mathlib's
652	    -- `Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`. The remaining substep work uses `s₀`
653	    -- (a `Finset`, hence `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀` is finite) to install the
654	    -- product-localisation commutation. The transport step from `s₀`-cover
655	    -- exactness to `s`-cover exactness is itself non-trivial: it requires
656	    -- a Čech-complex refinement comparison (`s₀ ⊆ s` ⇒ Čech-cohomology
657	    -- equality), which Mathlib does not currently expose directly but can
658	    -- be derived from the universal property of products via `Pi.eqOfPi`
659	    -- + the per-component (cofinal) refinement.
660	    have hs_fin : ∃ s' : Finset Γ(C.left, U), (↑s' : Set Γ(C.left, U)) ⊆ s ∧
661	        Ideal.span (↑s' : Set Γ(C.left, U)) = ⊤ :=
662	      (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite (s := s)).mp hs
663	    -- ===========================================================================
664	    -- Substep (b1-b2-c) merged with the iter-063 finite-subspanning data.
665	    -- ===========================================================================
666	    -- The route now has three components: (i) Čech-cohomology refinement
667	    -- transport `s → s₀` (using `hs_fin`); (ii) substep (b2) at the finite
668	    -- cover `s₀` where the product-localisation commutation succeeds; and
669	    -- (iii) `exact_of_isLocalized_span` on `s₀` together with the per-`f`
670	    -- slice-cover exactness `h_a_fun ⟨f, hs_fin.choose_spec.1 hf⟩` for
671	    -- `f ∈ s₀ ⊆ s`.
672	    --
673	    -- The Čech-refinement transport (i) is the new iter-064+ work; it has
674	    -- the form
675	    -- `Function.Exact (K(s).sc n).f (K(s).sc n).g ↔
676	    --    Function.Exact (K(s₀).sc n).f (K(s₀).sc n).g`
677	    -- under `hs_fin`. A natural framing: there is a *cochain-complex map*
678	    -- `K(s) → K(s₀)` (induced by the cofinal inclusion `s₀ ⊆ s` and the
679	    -- product universal property), and exactness transports along it
680	    -- iff one shows the map is a "Čech equivalence" — equivalently, by
681	    -- the lattice-supremum agreement `⨆ basicOpenCover s = U = ⨆ basicOpenCover s₀`,
682	    -- the two covers compute the same Čech cohomology via the
683	    -- `cechCohomology` carrier.
684	    -- ===========================================================================
685	    -- Iter-064: component (i) scaffolding — Čech-cohomology refinement transport.
686	    -- ===========================================================================
687	    -- Extract the finite subspanning set `s₀` and its properties.
688	    obtain ⟨s₀, h_sub, h_top⟩ := hs_fin
689	    -- Introduce shorthand for the two Čech cochain complexes.
690	    let K := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)
691	    let K₀ := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
692	      (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)))
693	    -- The inclusion `↑s₀ ⊆ s` induces a cochain-complex map `K → K₀` by
694	    -- precomposing multi-indices.  Exactness transports across this map because
695	    -- both covers generate the same sieve on `U` (`⨆ basicOpenCover s = U =
696	    -- ⨆ basicOpenCover ↑s₀`).  The transport is stated as a `have` below;
697	    -- closing it requires either a direct homological-algebra argument
698	    -- (quasi-isomorphism induced by the refinement) or a reduction to the
699	    -- sheaf-theoretic fact that Čech cohomology depends only on the generated
700	    -- sieve.  Mathlib does not yet expose this comparison directly.
701	    have h_transport :
702	      Function.Exact
703	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n).f)
704	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n).g) →
705	      Function.Exact
706	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K n).f)
707	        ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc K n).g) := by
708	      -- =========================================================================
709	      -- Iter-065 scaffolding: Čech-cohomology refinement transport `s → s₀`.
710	      -- =========================================================================
711	      intro h_exact_K₀
712	      -- Step 1: the inclusion `↑s₀ ↪ s` induces a map of index sets
713	      -- `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀ → Fin (i+1) → s` for each degree `i`.  By the universal
714	      -- property of the categorical product, this gives a projection map
715	      -- `π_i : K.X i → K₀.X i` in `ModuleCat k` (project onto the smaller index
716	      -- set).  These assemble into a cochain-complex map `π : K → K₀` because
717	      -- the Čech differential is natural in the index set.
718	      --
719	      -- **Iter-065 concrete construction** of `π`.  We assemble it through the
720	      -- Mathlib API:
721	      --   1. The inclusion `↑s₀ ⊆ s` gives a `FormalCoproduct`-morphism
722	      --      `g_FC : ⟨↑s₀, basicOpenCover ↑s₀⟩ ⟶ ⟨s, basicOpenCover s⟩`
723	      --      (function `j ↦ ⟨j.1, h_sub j.2⟩` on indices, identity on objects
724	      --      since `basicOpenCover s ⟨j.1, _⟩ = C.left.basicOpen j.1 =
725	      --      basicOpenCover ↑s₀ j`).
726	      --   2. `Limits.FormalCoproduct.cechFunctor` applied to `g_FC` produces a
727	      --      morphism of simplicial objects in `FormalCoproduct (Opens _)`.
728	      --   3. `cosimplicialObjectFunctor` precomposes with `_.rightOp`, hence is
729	      --      *contravariant* in the simplicial object.  Applying it via
730	      --      `whiskeringLeft` yields a natural transformation
731	      --      `cosimplicialObjectFunctor (mk s _).cech ⟶
732	      --       cosimplicialObjectFunctor (mk ↑s₀ _).cech`.
733	      --   4. Whiskering with `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` and evaluating at
734	      --      the underlying presheaf of `toModuleKSheaf C` gives the desired
735	      --      cochain-complex morphism `K ⟶ K₀`.
736	      let g_FC :
737	          (Limits.FormalCoproduct.mk (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U))
738	              (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)))) ⟶
739	          (Limits.FormalCoproduct.mk s
740	              (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) :=
741	        { f := fun j => ⟨j.1, h_sub j.2⟩
742	          φ := fun _ => 𝟙 _ }
743	      let g_simp :
744	          (Limits.FormalCoproduct.mk (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U))
745	              (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U)
746	                (↑s₀ : Set Γ(C.left, U)))).cech ⟶
747	          (Limits.FormalCoproduct.mk s
748	              (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)).cech :=
749	        Limits.FormalCoproduct.cechFunctor.map g_FC
750	      let π : HomologicalComplex.Hom K K₀ :=
751	        ((Functor.whiskerLeft (Limits.FormalCoproduct.evalOp _ (ModuleCat.{u} k))
752	            ((Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).map g_simp.rightOp)) ◫
753	          𝟙 (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k))).app
754	          ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (toModuleKSheaf C))
755	      -- Step 2: each component `π_i` is a split surjection in `ModuleCat k`.
756	      -- The splitting is given by extending a function `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀` to
757	      -- `Fin (i+1) → s` by choosing arbitrary values on the complement of `↑s₀`
758	      -- (possible because `s` is non-empty, as `Ideal.span s = ⊤`).
759	      --
760	      -- Iter-066: the categorical content is captured by
761	      -- `splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective` (defined above), which says a product
762	      -- projection induced by an injection of index sets is split in
763	      -- `ModuleCat k`. The remaining obligation is to identify `π.f i` as such
764	      -- a product projection — namely
765	      -- `Pi.lift (fun j' : Fin(i+1) → ↑s₀ => Pi.π M (g_FC.f ∘ j'))` for the
766	      -- family `M : (Fin(i+1) → s) → ModuleCat k` given by
767	      -- `j ↦ F.obj (op (∏ᶜ_a basicOpenCover s (j a)))`. From the simp lemmas of
768	      -- `evalOp` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean` L383),
769	      -- of `cechFunctor` (`.../FormalCoproducts/Cech.lean` L194), and of
770	      -- `alternatingCofaceMapComplex`, `π.f i` reduces to exactly this form
771	      -- (the `≫ F.map (𝟙).op` factor collapses by `F.map_id` since
772	      -- `(powerMap g_FC (Fin (i+1))).φ j' = 𝟙` for our `g_FC`).
773	      --
774	      -- Decomposing into named subobligations:
775	      have h_inj : Function.Injective (g_FC.f) := fun a b hab => by
776	        -- g_FC.f j = ⟨j.1, h_sub j.2⟩ ∈ s; injective because Subtype.val is.
777	        have : (g_FC.f a).1 = (g_FC.f b).1 := congrArg Subtype.val hab
778	        exact Subtype.ext this
779	      have h_inj' (i : ℕ) :
780	          Function.Injective (fun (j' : Fin (i + 1) → ↑s₀) => g_FC.f ∘ j') :=
781	        fun a b hab => funext (fun x => h_inj (congrFun hab x))
782	      -- The actual identification of `π.f i` with the product projection (and
783	      -- thus the SplitEpi) remains to be carried out. The helper
784	      -- `splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective` reduces the work to this identification
785	      -- step. See `task_results/BasicOpenCech.lean.md` for the iter-066
786	      -- analysis of the simp lemmas needed.
787	      have h_π_split (i : ℕ) : SplitEpi (π.f i) := by
788	        -- Approach: use convert with the helper, then close the equality subgoal
789	        -- via simp lemmas for evalOp, cechFunctor, and alternatingCofaceMapComplex.
790	        convert splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective _ (fun j' => g_FC.f ∘ j') (h_inj' i) using 2
791	        · -- After convert, the goal is π.f i = Pi.lift (...).  Simplify the
792	          -- left-hand side using the simps lemmas for evalOp, cechFunctor,
793	          -- and alternatingCofaceMapComplex, then unfold g_simp and use the
794	          -- powerMap simps.  The remaining identity morphisms are eliminated
795	          -- pointwise after congr+funext.
796	          simp [π]
797	          rw [show g_simp = FormalCoproduct.cechFunctor.map g_FC by rfl]
798	          simp [FormalCoproduct.cechFunctor_map_app, FormalCoproduct.powerMap_f,
799	            FormalCoproduct.powerMap_φ]

have h_diff_pi_smul_g : ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₂ i),
          letI := h_mod_pi₂
          letI := h_mod_pi₃
          e₃ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (e₂.symm (r • y))) =
            r • e₃ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (e₂.symm y)) := by
        -- Same content as `h_diff_pi_smul_f` at one degree shifted (see the
        -- iter-073 prover analysis comment-block above for the full reduction
        -- recipe; `scK₀.g = K₀.d n (n+1)` follows the same `AlternatingCofaceMapComplex`
        -- → `Pi.lift (Pi.π ≫ presheaf.map)` shape, and each restriction is R-linear

input.content:
# Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean — iter-076 repair lane

## Outcome

- **File compiles cleanly**: zero errors. The 5 errors flagged by the plan agent
  (L412 whnf timeout, L952 unsolved goals on `h_mod_X₃`, L1088 simp no progress,
  L1170–1171 application type mismatch, L1182 isDefEq timeout, plus the
  cascading L1205 unsolved goals + L1213 whnf timeout) are all resolved.
- **Sorry count**: 6 → **7** (within the plan's `≤ 9` budget; +1 over baseline).
- **No new axioms.**
- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (per user feedback policy).

## Sorries — old vs new mapping

Original 6 (before iter-076):
- L495 — substep (a) for `s` (slice-cover extra-degeneracy)
- L819 — kernel-π acyclicity (Step 3 of `h_transport`)
- L847 — substep (a) for `s₀`
- L1166 — h_diff_pi_smul_f's sign-free per-summand R-linearity
- L1181 — h_diff_pi_smul_g body
- L1260 — h_loc_exact (Step 4)

New 7 (after iter-076):
- L495 — unchanged
- L819 — unchanged
- L847 — unchanged
- L1077 — h_diff_pi_smul_f body (rolled back from the L1083+ chain)
- **L1106 — f_R.map_smul' body (NEW; was a `calc` chain that depended on `h_diff_pi_smul_f` + literal `Module R scK₀.X₁` instance equality)**
- **L1116 — g_R.map_smul' body (NEW)**
- L1145 — h_loc_exact (was L1260; line shift from edits above)

Removed: the entire `h_diff_pi_smul_g` declaration (its statement could not
type-check because `scK₀.X₃` does not reduce to `↑(∏ᶜ Z₃)` — see L952 analysis
below — and it was only used by `g_R.map_smul'`, which is now rolled back too).

## Per-error analysis

### L952 — `h_mod_X₃` unsolved `Module = Module` after `convert h`

**Root cause.** `scK₀.X₃ = (HomologicalComplex.sc K₀ n).X₃ = K₀.X
((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next n)`.  The `ComplexShape.next` function is **opaque**
(defined via `Classical.choose` over the `Rel` predicate), so `(ComplexShape.up
ℕ).next n` does **not** reduce to `n + 1` by `rfl` — you need
`CochainComplex.next` (Mathlib lemma: `(ComplexShape.up α).next i = i + 1`).
That's why `convert h` succeeds verbatim for X₁ and X₂ (whose `Fin` indices use
the literal `prev n + 1` / `n + 1`) but fails for X₃ (whose `Fin (next n + 1)`
index doesn't auto-reduce to match `Z₃`'s `Fin (n + 2)`).

**Repair (kept the iter-071/072 structural transport plan intact).**

```lean
have h_mod_X₃ : Module R scK₀.X₃ := by
  have h_eq : scK₀.X₃ = K₀.X (n + 1) := by
    show K₀.X ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).next n) = K₀.X (n + 1)
    rw [CochainComplex.next]
  rw [h_eq]
  dsimp [K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]
  letI := h_mod_pi₃
  have h : Module R ↑(∏ᶜ Z₃) := e₃.toAddEquiv.module R
  convert h
```

This rewrites the `next n` to `n + 1` at the type level *before* the dsimp,
making the subsequent `convert h` succeed exactly as in the X₁/X₂ pattern.

### L1088 — `simp only [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply, Finset.smul_sum]` made no progress

After the 5-layer functor-stack unfold at L1080, the goal is *componentwise*
(post-`funext j`) and the `Pi.smul_apply / Finset.sum_apply / Finset.smul_sum`
lemmas don't fire (the indexed-sum shape isn't exposed by the 5-layer simp set,
contrary to iter-075's hopeful framing).

**Initial repair (kept):** wrap in `try` so the chain doesn't fail.

**Follow-on cascade (L1095).** Once `try simp only [...]` is benign, the next
tactic `refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun k _ => ?_` fails because the goal is
`_ j = _ j` (a componentwise equality), not `∑ = ∑`.  The entire iter-075
chain (funext + simp + refine + rw + congr + sorry) only works if the 5-layer
unfold actually exposes alternating-sum form, which it doesn't.

**Final repair.** Roll back h_diff_pi_smul_f body to `intro r y; sorry`,
preserving the iter-073 / iter-075 / iter-076 recipe comments (S1–S8 + sign-peel
+ sign-free 5-step) for the next iteration.

### L1170–1171 — `h_diff_pi_smul_g` application type mismatch

The statement uses `e₃ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (e₂.symm (r • y)))`,
but `(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.g) (...) : ↑scK₀.X₃` and `e₃` expects
`↑(∏ᶜ Z₃)`.  These are *not* defeq (same `next n ≠ n + 1` opaqueness as
L952).  Unlike `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (where `↑scK₀.X₂` *is* defeq to `↑(∏ᶜ Z₂)`
because `n` is literal in both), the g-variant cannot type-check without an
explicit `CochainComplex.next` rewrite — which would require the rewrite to
be inside the statement type, not the proof body.

**Repair.** Drop the `h_diff_pi_smul_g` declaration entirely.  It was only
used by `g_R.map_smul'`, which is rolled back to `sorry` in the same edit.
The intent + reduction recipe survives in the (commented) `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
block above, and the rollback note at the call site documents what the
next-iteration prover needs to re-establish.

### L1182 — isDefEq heartbeat ≥ 200000

Cascade from the L1170–1171 elaboration failure; resolved by dropping the
declaration.

### L412 — whnf heartbeat

Cascade from the L1170–1182 / L1205+ chain inside
`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`.  Resolved automatically
once the inner declarations are rolled back.

### L1205 — unsolved `(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f)(r • x) = (ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f)(e₁.symm (r • e₁ x))`

`congr 1` in the f_R calc chain expected `r • x` and `e₁.symm (r • e₁ x)` to
be definitionally equal — but the `Module R scK₀.X₁` instance found via
`h_mod_X₁` (which was built with `convert h`) is *not* the literal term
`e₁.toAddEquiv.module R`; `convert` introduces a residual congruence that
breaks the literal-equality `rfl`.

### L1213 — isDefEq heartbeat in f_R calc final step

Cascade from the failed `congr 1` above.  The calc chain `r • F(x) =
e₂.symm (r • e₂ F(x))` then tries an `Eq.refl` via the same instance-equality
issue, hitting heartbeats during the implicit unification.

**Repair (L1205+L1213+the g-variant L1214+).** Roll back `f_R.map_smul'` and
`g_R.map_smul'` to `intro r x; sorry`.  The structural-transport intent is
preserved in the rollback comments; the next iteration's prover must
re-build `h_mod_X_i` to produce a *literal* `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R`
instance (e.g., via explicit `Eq.mpr` casting on a manual `↑scK₀.X_i =
↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` equality) so that the `r • x = e_i.symm (r • e_i x)` step
closes by `rfl`.

## Diagnostics after repair

```
{"success":true, "items": [warnings only — no errors]}
```

The warnings are:
- `declaration uses sorry` at lines 453, 819, 847, 1077, 1106, 1116, 1145
  (expected — 7 active sorries, all carrying explicit ITER-076 rollback
  comments + reduction-recipe documentation for the next iteration).
- A few `simp [π]` "flexible tactic" linter warnings at lines 796–803
  (pre-existing).
- A handful of `linter.style.longLine` warnings at lines 984–1065
  (pre-existing comments).
- One `linter.style.show` warning at L960 (my `show K₀.X ((ComplexShape.up
  ℕ).next n) = K₀.X (n + 1)` is changing the goal — but the rewrite that
  follows REQUIRES the explicit form, so I'm keeping it).

## Blueprint markers

No blueprint editing this iteration (per task scope: prover writes results, not
blueprint).  The blueprint file
`blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex § Čech acyclicity ...`
already covers this content; the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase between
prover and review will adjust `\leanok` based on the new sorry counts.

## Repair budget

- Starts: 6 sorries.
- Plan budget: at most 9.
- Ends: 7 sorries (+1 over baseline; -2 from the original budget allowance).

## Next-iteration prover handoff

The hard blocker for the four rolled-back proof bodies (`h_diff_pi_smul_f`,
`f_R.map_smul'`, `g_R.map_smul'`, `h_loc_exact`) is **instance literality**:
the `convert h` (and `convert h using N`) constructions of `h_mod_X_i` produce
instances that differ from the canonical `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R` by a
residual eta-congruence (in X₃'s case, by an opaque `CochainComplex.next`
unfold).  The next iteration's prover should:

1. Rebuild `h_mod_X_i` via explicit `Eq.mpr` over a manual `↑scK₀.X_i =
   ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i)` equality, so `(Module R scK₀.X_i).toSMul` equals
   `e_i.toEquiv.smul R` *literally*.
2. With that in hand, the iter-072 `calc` chains in `f_R.map_smul'` /
   `g_R.map_smul'` work as written (the `congr 1` for `r • x = e_i.symm
   (r • e_i x)` will close by `rfl` because the smul comes from
   `e_i.toEquiv.smul R`).
3. Then `h_diff_pi_smul_f` can be re-attacked via the documented S1–S8 recipe
   (decomposition of the 5-layer functor stack into per-summand R-linearity).
4. `h_loc_exact` is downstream of those.

goals` at the enclosing `by` block.  The same pattern recurred with `convert
h using {4,5,6}` and `congr! 1` — all empty-diagnostics, all leaving
unsolved goals after the actual edit.

Root cause hypothesis: `multi_attempt`'s `diagnostics` filter is
*line-scoped* and the "unsolved goals" diagnostic only attaches to the `by`
opener line (here L952 col 43), not the line containing the snippet
(L956+).  So empty-diagnostics on the snippet's line is a *necessary* but
not *sufficient* indicator of goal closure.

Recommended UX fix: have `multi_attempt` additionally report the goal state
*after* the snippet (in a `goals_after` field separate from `goals` /
`goals_before`).  Empty `goals_after` = success; non-empty = failure, even
if `diagnostics` is empty.

(I worked around this by editing-then-checking, but a 2x feedback-loop cost.)

EOF
echo "Feedback noted."

content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

Repair complete. Summary:

**`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean`** now compiles cleanly with **zero errors**. Sorry count went from 6 → 7 (within the plan's `≤ 9` budget).

Key repairs:
- **L952 (`h_mod_X₃`)**: Pre-rewrote `scK₀.X₃ = K₀.X (n + 1)` via `CochainComplex.next` so the subsequent `convert h` matches the `Z₃` index shape literally. The X₁/X₂ cases worked without rewrite because `prev n + 1` / `n + 1` are literal in their `Z_i` definitions; `next n` is opaque (defined via `Classical.choose`).
- **L1088/L1095 (h_diff_pi_smul_f chain)**: Rolled back the iter-075 `funext + 5-layer simp + refine Finset.sum_congr + smul_comm + congr + sorry` chain to `intro r y; sorry`. The `simp only [Pi.smul_apply, Finset.sum_apply, Finset.smul_sum]` doesn't fire because the 5-layer unfold doesn't expose alternating-sum form, and the subsequent `refine` then fails on the `_ j = _ j` shape. Recipe comments preserved.
- **L1170–1172/L1182 (h_diff_pi_smul_g)**: Dropped the declaration entirely — its statement was un-elaborable because `scK₀.X₃` doesn't reduce to `↑(∏ᶜ Z₃)` (same root cause as L952).
- **L1205/L1213/L412 (f_R/g_R `map_smul'`)**: Rolled back both calc chains to `intro r x; sorry`. The `congr 1` for `r • x = e_i.symm (r • e_i x)` doesn't close because `h_mod_X_i` (built via `convert`) is not the literal `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R` instance. L412 was a cascade.

Task results written to `.archon/task_results/BasicOpenCech.lean.md` with full per-error analysis and a next-iteration handoff (rebuild `h_mod_X_i` via explicit `Eq.mpr` so the smul is literally `e_i.toEquiv.smul R`).
